introSection: Rockin' Roller was a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Six Flags St. Louis in Eureka, Missouri, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The roller coaster opened in 1975 at Six Flags Over Mid-America as Rock Candy Express. It was located in the Goodtime Hollow children's area.

In 1985, the coaster was given a Looney Tunes theme and was renamed the Acme Gravity Powered Roller Ride, with the area becoming known as Looney Tunes Town. The coaster was themed to the characters Wile E. Coyote and Road Runner.

After the 2005 season, the Looney Tunes Town area was disbanded in order to make room for the new Bugs Bunny National Park children's area. The coaster was moved to the former spot where Looney Tooter previously stood. This was done to make space for the re-track and expansion of Moon Antique Cars. The coaster was renamed to Rockin' Roller in its new location, stripped of its Looney Tunes theming.

The coaster remained standing but not operating during the 2007 season and was removed late in the year along with the area to make way for Evel Knievel in 2008. The coaster was then stored in the employee parking lot. Partway through the 2008 season, it was removed from the park and was scrapped.

fullText:
Rockin' Roller was a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Six Flags St. Louis in Eureka, Missouri, USA.

The roller coaster opened in 1975 at Six Flags Over Mid-America as Rock Candy Express. It was located in the Goodtime Hollow children's area.

In 1985, the coaster was given a Looney Tunes theme and was renamed the Acme Gravity Powered Roller Ride, with the area becoming known as Looney Tunes Town. The coaster was themed to the characters Wile E. Coyote and Road Runner.

After the 2005 season, the Looney Tunes Town area was disbanded in order to make room for the new Bugs Bunny National Park children's area. The coaster was moved to the former spot where Looney Tooter previously stood. This was done to make space for the re-track and expansion of Moon Antique Cars. The coaster was renamed to Rockin' Roller in its new location, stripped of its Looney Tunes theming.

The coaster remained standing but not operating during the 2007 season and was removed late in the year along with the area to make way for Evel Knievel in 2008. The coaster was then stored in the employee parking lot. Partway through the 2008 season, it was removed from the park and was scrapped.



Black track and supports.
